executive_summary: Total department sales for the week across available departments reached 25,111.61 ex VAT; full store sales including Cigarettes, Deli, Produce, and Provisions are not available this period, limiting a complete P&L view.

Purchases data is missing across all departments, resulting in COGS calculations showing only negative closing stock values rather than true cost of sales; this is a critical data integrity issue requiring immediate investigation.
Reported margins across all meat and fish departments are significantly above 100%, confirming that the absence of purchases data has rendered the P&L figures unreliable for this week.
Total waste stands at 8,719.80, which is a material figure; without confirmed sales totals, waste percentage cannot be accurately calculated, but this level of waste is a significant cost concern.
Fish waste of 1,306.99 and Deli waste of 1,359.55 are the two highest individual department waste figures and together account for approximately 30.5% of total store waste.
Poultry waste of 1,031.56 and Provisions waste of 1,006.37 are also notably high, each exceeding 1,000.00, and warrant immediate review.
Scan margins across available departments range from 13.94% (Bacon and Loose SRP) to 24.75% (Lamb), which appear reasonable benchmarks once purchases data is restored and true GP% can be compared.
A full data audit is required before this week's P&L can be signed off; key missing inputs include all department purchases, opening stocks, and full sales figures for Cigarettes, Deli, and Produce.
biggest_wins: Lamb achieved the highest scan margin of the week at 24.75% on sales of 984.76, indicating strong theoretical profitability relative to its category peers.
Beef generated the highest absolute sales among individual meat departments at 10,969.49, making it the strongest revenue contributor in the available data set.
Poultry delivered sales of 8,220.26, the second highest of all reported departments, demonstrating consistent volume performance in the fresh protein category.
Fish recorded sales of 2,220.01 with a scan margin of 15.21%, and while waste at 1,306.99 is a concern, the sales base provides headroom to improve net contribution through waste reduction.
Pork and Bacon combined contributed 2,717.09 in sales, providing solid category breadth alongside the larger meat departments.
biggest_risks: Purchases data is missing for every department, making it impossible to calculate true COGS, gross profit, or GP% for this week; all reported margins are meaningless in their current state and must not be used for decision-making.
Total waste of 8,719.80 is a significant cost exposure; without confirmed sales, the true waste percentage is unknown, but the absolute figure alone demands urgent category-level review.

Deli waste of 1,359.55 is the highest single department waste figure and suggests either over-production, poor date management, or both; there is no Deli sales figure available to contextualise the waste ratio.

Fish waste of 1,306.99 is exceptionally high and likely represents a substantial portion of Fish sales of 2,220.01; if waste were approximately 58.9% of sales in this department, it would indicate a critical ordering and rotation failure.
Poultry waste of 1,031.56 against sales of 8,220.26 equates to approximately 12.5% waste on sales, which is above acceptable norms and requires review of ordering patterns and shelf life management.

Provisions waste of 1,006.37 is notable given the closing stock of only 24.18 and the absence of sales and purchases data for the department; the waste figure may indicate a clearance event or a stock write-off that needs to be documented.

Produce closing stock of 8,152.47 with no sales or purchases figures available means the department's performance is entirely opaque; combined with Produce waste of 1,515.21, this is a high-risk area.
Bacon and Loose SRP recorded the lowest scan margin at 13.94% on sales of 1,349.36, indicating limited theoretical profitability in this sub-category before waste and shrink are factored in.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: No data available for sales, purchases, opening stock, closing stock, COGS, profit, or margin this week; full department data must be sourced before any performance assessment is possible.

Beef: Sales of 10,969.49 with a scan margin of 16.38%; waste of 594.35 requires monitoring but is proportionally lower than several other departments relative to sales volume; purchases data needed to confirm true GP.
Lamb: Sales of 984.76 with the strongest scan margin in the data set at 24.75%; waste of 149.87 is the lowest among the meat departments; purchases data required to validate true profitability.
Pork: Sales of 1,367.73 with a scan margin of 18.68%; waste of 99.78 is well controlled and the lowest absolute waste figure in the fresh meat category.
Bacon and Loose SRP: Sales of 1,349.36 with the weakest scan margin at 13.94%; waste of 97.78 is manageable but the thin margin leaves little room for shrink or write-offs.
Poultry: Sales of 8,220.26 with a scan margin of 16.05%; waste of 1,031.56 is a concern and equates to approximately 12.5% of sales, which is above acceptable levels and must be addressed.
Fish: Sales of 2,220.01 with a scan margin of 15.21%; waste of 1,306.99 is critically high relative to the sales figure and suggests serious issues with ordering volume, rotation, or both.

Produce: No sales or purchases figures available; closing stock of 8,152.47 and waste of 1,515.21 indicate a high-value department with significant stock and shrink exposure that is currently unmonitored in this report.

Deli: No sales or purchases figures available; waste of 1,359.55 is the highest individual department waste figure in the store and cannot be contextualised without sales data; this is an urgent reporting gap.

Provisions: No sales or purchases figures available; closing stock of only 24.18 alongside waste of 1,006.37 is an unusual combination that may indicate a significant write-off or clearance event requiring documentation.
Dairy: No sales, purchases, or margin data available; waste of 491.53 is recorded and requires review against sales performance once data is obtained.

Bread and Cakes: No sales, purchases, or margin data available; waste of 1,003.18 is a material figure and likely reflects end-of-day markdown failures or over-ordering; must be reviewed against ordering and production schedules.
Frozen: Waste of 63.64 is the lowest category waste figure in the store and represents well-controlled shrink in this department.
action_plan: Urgently contact the back-office or accounts team to identify why purchases data has not populated for any department this week; the P&L cannot be signed off or acted upon without this information.
Obtain full sales figures for Cigarettes, Deli, Produce, and Provisions so that a complete store sales total and waste percentage can be calculated for the week ending 2026-06-13.

Conduct an immediate review of Fish ordering quantities and delivery schedules; waste of 1,306.99 against sales of 2,220.01 suggests the department is being over-ordered by a material amount and corrective action is required this week.

Review Deli production schedules and end-of-day waste logs to identify the root cause of 1,359.55 in waste; implement or reinforce a daily production review with the Deli manager to align output with trading patterns.

Investigate the Provisions department anomaly where waste of 1,006.37 is recorded against a closing stock of only 24.18 with no sales or purchases; determine whether this reflects a write-off, a clearance, or a data entry error and document accordingly.

Review Poultry ordering and rotation procedures; waste of 1,031.56 representing approximately 12.5% of sales of 8,220.26 is above target and incremental reductions through tighter ordering would have a direct impact on department profitability.
Review Bread and Cakes production or order quantities in light of waste of 1,003.18; ensure end-of-day markdown procedures are being followed consistently to convert potential waste into revenue.

Assess Produce stock management practices given closing stock of 8,152.47 and waste of 1,515.21; a full stock rotation check should be carried out and ordering reviewed against current sales velocity.
Ensure Dairy waste of 491.53 is reviewed against current sales and date code management; confirm that FEFO rotation is being applied consistently across all chilled dairy lines.
Use the available scan margins as interim benchmarks for Beef at 16.38%, Poultry at 16.05%, and Fish at 15.21% to assess promotional activity and pricing decisions until true GP figures are restored.

Schedule a data integrity review with the store's systems administrator to understand why opening stock and purchases fields are returning zero across all departments and implement a process to flag such anomalies automatically before the report is generated.

Once purchases data is recovered, recalculate all department margins and compare actual GP% against scan margins to identify the departments with the greatest gap, and prioritise waste reduction and shrink control in those areas first.
